### Post by jimbob8472 on 2007-06-29
Hi 
   I'm having trouble with my screen resolution in Feisty. I wish to set my resolution to 1280x1024 however i cant set it above 1280x800 (which is a wide screen resolution). I have tried editing the xorg.conf file, but had no luck. 

i have a GeForce 7100 GS GFXcard and the latest nvidia drivers installed using Envy 


here is my xorg.conf file 

# nvidia-xconfig: X configuration file generated by nvidia-xconfig
# nvidia-xconfig:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Wed Jun 13 18:39:30 PDT 2007

# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 1.0  (buildmeister@builder3)  Mon Feb 26 23:39:38 PST 2007

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Files"
    RgbPath         "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load           "dbe"
    Load           "extmod"
    Load           "type1"
    Load           "freetype"
    Load           "glx"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

    # HorizSync source: xconfig, VertRefresh source: xconfig
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "CRT-0"
    HorizSync       28.0 - 51.0
    VertRefresh     43.0 - 60.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Videocard0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce 7100 GS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"

    #Option         "metamodes" "1024x768_60 +0+0"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Videocard0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
    Option         "TripleBuffer" "true"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
        Modes      "1600x1200" "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
    Option         "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

### Post by imluxwhoru on 2007-06-29
I can help ya with that one. But, 1st I need to know the brand and model number of your monitor. 

Also, your xorg.conf is a little goofy. We'll fix that too when we fix your resolution.  :)

...Oh yea, and what version of Ubuntu are you using? (Ex... Ubuntu Feisty, Kubuntu Dapper... etc)

### Post by imluxwhoru on 2007-06-30
Ok. The problem you were having was due to the VertRefresh & HorizSync lines in the "Monitor" Section. I corrected these values to be consistent with (most) Dell TFT Active Matrix 19" Monitors. It should do the trick. 

I also took the liberty of cleaning up and re-arranging your xorg.conf. Everything is now in proper order, and easy to read!! I also changed the Identifiers of your graphics system so you will know what stuff is when you look at the file. (What can I say... I'm a perfectionist. :))

We're going to back up your xorg.conf before we do anything... So, open up a terminal and:
```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
```

Once that is done, all ya do is open up /etc/X11/xorg.conf in your favorite text editor (I usually use pico, but Gedit is just as good I suppose), Delete all the existing code and paste all this goop in there. Save and exit the text editor. Then just restart X ("Ctrl+Alt+Backspace") and you should be looking at a pretty 1280x1024 screen res.

```

# IMLuxWhoRU XConfig tool. Generated by IMLuxWhoRU on June 30th 2007.
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
     RgbPath "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"
EndSection

Section "Module"
     Load "dbe"
     Load "extmod"
     Load "type1"
     Load "freetype"
     Load "glx"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
     Identifier "Mouse0"
     Driver "mouse"
     Option "Protocol" "auto"
     Option "Device" "/dev/psaux"
     Option "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
     Option "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
     Identifier "Keyboard0"
     Driver "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
     Identifier "Dell TFT"
     VendorName "Unknown"
     ModelName "CRT-0"
     HorizSync 31.0 - 81.0 
     VertRefresh 56.0 - 76.0
     Option "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
     Identifier "nVidia GeForce 7100 GS"
     Driver "nvidia"
     VendorName "NVIDIA Corporation"
     BoardName "GeForce 7100 GS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
     Identifier "Default Screen"
     Device "nVidia GeForce 7100 GS"
     Monitor "Dell TFT"
     DefaultDepth 24
     Option "AddARGBGLXVisuals" "True"
     Option "TripleBuffer" "true"
          SubSection "Display"
          Depth 24
          Modes "1280x1024"
     EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
     Identifier "Layout0"
     Screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
     InputDevice "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
     InputDevice "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
     Option "Composite" "Enable"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
     Option "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection
```

If something goes wrong, and X wont start right, just "Ctrl+Alt+F1" into a terminial if you're not already looking at one, and do this.

```
sudo mv /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.broke
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup /etc/X11/xorg.conf
sudo startx
```

That will undo everything we did (while still saving the new xorg.conf) and let you get back into the GUI. Then just let me know what happened and we'll go from there.

Good luck!

---

### Post by jimbob8472 on 2007-06-30

Its nice to see that the community is good with Ubuntu 

Thanks once again

### Post by imluxwhoru on 2007-06-30
w00t!! Glad to be of service.

Yes, the community is phenomenal with Ubuntu. That is why I got it, and still use it! If it wasn't for the community support, my computer wouldn't be able to do the sweet and insane things that it does, and I wouldn't know half as much as I do. 

Ubuntu is the future!

Oh, and if you have the desire to go to a different res, all you have to do is change the "1280 X 1024" in the Screen section to whatever res you're after. You should be able to get all the way to 1200 x 1600!! 

Have fun!
